query: South Korea KOSPI foreign investor net buy/sell value weekly latest June 2026, recent trend in trillion won
answer:
I've completed a thorough research report on KOSPI foreign investor net buy/sell activity. Here are the key findings:

## Summary

**Latest data (through June 29 – July 1, 2026):** South Korea's KOSPI saw a **record foreign investor sell-off** in H1 2026, even as the index itself soared ~101%.

**Headline figures (in trillion won):**
- **H1 2026 total foreign net selling: –148.32 trillion won** (~$95–107B) — a record, sometimes rounded to "nearly 150 trillion won"
- **June 29, 2026: –7.756 trillion won** — a single-day record net sell (largest since records began in 1998); ~7.76T also cited around July 1
- **May–June (2 months): –92.89 trillion won** — accelerating phase
- **March 2026: –35+ trillion won**

**Recent trend:** Heavy, sustained selling with one notable interruption — a **+1.7 trillion won net buy on June 12** (first buy in 25 sessions, on Middle East ceasefire hopes), after which selling resumed and intensified into month-end.

**Who absorbed it:** Retail investors bought +99.17T won; institutions +35.05T won in H1.

**Drivers:** Profit-taking/rebalancing after the enormous rally, plus won depreciation (from ~1,483 to ~1,555–1,559/$). Selling was ~92% concentrated in chips (Samsung, SK Hynix), dropping foreign ownership of Samsung to ~47% (16.5-year low).

**Caveat:** No confirmed *specific July-week* aggregate was available as of July 2, 2026; the most granular recent figures are daily (June 29–July 1). The official real-time source is the Korea Exchange (KRX) Information Data System.

The full structured output with all data points and sources has been submitted.

sources.title: Foreign investors' record Kospi sell-off may continue in second half as won weakens
sources.snippet: A record 148 trillion won ($95 billion) in net selling by foreign investors during the first half; net sales of 148.32 trillion won in the Kospi market.

sources.title: Foreigners Dump $106.8 Billion of Kospi Stocks in First Half as Samsung Ownership Hits 16-Year Low
sources.snippet: Foreign investors were net sellers of 148.316 trillion won on the Kospi in H1; June 29 single-day record of 7.756 trillion won; Samsung ownership 47%, SK Hynix 50%.

sources.title: Foreign Investors Dump 150 Trillion Won in KOSPI in First Half
sources.snippet: KOSPI closed at 8,476.48 on June 30, up 81.83 points (0.97%); foreign investors dumped nearly 150 trillion won in H1.

sources.title: Foreign Investors Return After a Month... Net Buying 1.7 Trillion Won in KOSPI
sources.snippet: June 12 foreign net buying of ~1.7 trillion won, first in 25 sessions; preceding 24 sessions saw 75.569 trillion won net sales.

sources.title: Kospi, SK Hynix, Samsung Electronics: Why foreign investors are selling
sources.snippet: On Monday overseas investors unloaded a net 1.24 trillion won (~$801M) of Kospi-listed shares per Korea Exchange data.

sources.title: Kospi selloff is rebalancing, not vote against South Korea: KRX CEO
sources.snippet: KRX CEO Jeong Eun-bo: Kospi grew 76% in 2025, up 108.85% YTD before selloff began June 3; selling is rebalancing.

sources.title: Won nears 1,560 on strong dollar, foreign stock selling
sources.snippet: Won-dollar closed at 1,554.9 (July 1); KOSPI ended 2.04% lower at 8,303.41.

sources.title: Foreign Investors Return to KOSPI After 12-Day Selling Streak
sources.snippet: Foreigners sold more than 35 trillion won in March alone; turned net buyers after 12 trading days.

sources.title: KRX Information Data System — Trading by Investor
sources.snippet: Official Korea Exchange data on trading by investor type (Sell/Buy/Net Buying), values in KRW 1 billion, ~20-min delayed.
